calendar.matters:
Petition To Determine Entitlement To Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-18-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sheila Robello for and with (trustee) Charles Lane and for Peter Siggins. Josephina Tarantino-Versace in Propria Persona. Rosemary Rinauro (mother) also present.

Ms. Robello reported that a response was filed, that there are procedural issues, that a probate has not been opened, that there is a claim of an omitted child, that there was knowledge of the child prior to execution of the trust, that there are issues of timely notice, that more than 120 days had past and that there is no basis to grant this petition. Ms. Tarantino-Versace stated that she does not have and cannot afford an attorney, that she did not know that her father had died, that the trustee will not talk to her without an attorney and that she is asking for justice. Ms. Rinauro stated that she believes that the will and trust were not amended properly, that the codicil does not contain verbiage as such and that it does not specify a disinheritance. Ms. Robello stated that there is no challenge to the paternity. Ms. Rinauro stated that the codicils say one thing, the trust says another and that it does not reference disinheritance. Ms. Tarantino-Versace stated that she is looking for justice, that Mr. Lane is taking the law into his own hands, that in 2004 he took away the child support and she could not finish school and that the DNA tests were done so that this could not happen. The petition to determine entitlement to distribution was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)
